name: node-security
description: Node.js and JavaScript security patterns and common vulnerabilities

observations.title: Always validate and sanitize user input at boundaries
observations.narrative: Never trust input from HTTP requests, WebSocket messages, or CLI arguments. Use Zod, Joi, or similar to validate shape and types before processing.

observations.title: Prototype pollution via object spread and Object.assign
observations.narrative: Merging user-controlled objects with Object.assign or spread can overwrite __proto__. Use Object.create(null) for lookup objects, or validate keys before merging.

observations.title: Use parameterized queries — never interpolate SQL
observations.narrative: Template literals in SQL queries enable SQL injection. Always use parameterized queries: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]).

observations.title: Store secrets in environment variables, never in code
observations.narrative: API keys, database passwords, and tokens must come from env vars or a secret manager. Add .env to .gitignore. Use dotenv or platform-native secret injection.

observations.title: Set security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options
observations.narrative: Configure Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, and X-Frame-Options on all HTTP responses. Use helmet middleware in Express.

observations.title: SSRF risk from user-supplied URLs in server fetch
observations.narrative: If your server fetches URLs from user input, attackers can reach internal services (169.254.169.254, localhost). Validate URLs against an allowlist of domains and block private IP ranges.

observations.title: Use bcrypt or argon2 for password hashing
observations.narrative: Never store passwords with SHA-256 or MD5 — they're too fast. Use bcrypt (cost factor 10+) or argon2id. These are deliberately slow and resistant to GPU attacks.

observations.title: Rate limit authentication endpoints
observations.narrative: Apply strict rate limiting (e.g., 5 attempts per minute per IP) to login, registration, and password reset endpoints. Use sliding window counters, not simple token buckets.

observations.title: Path traversal in file operations
observations.narrative: User-controlled file paths like '../../../etc/passwd' can escape intended directories. Always resolve paths with path.resolve() and verify the result starts with your allowed directory.

observations.title: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual for secret comparison
observations.narrative: Comparing secrets with === is vulnerable to timing attacks. Use crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(a), Buffer.from(b)) for constant-time comparison of tokens and signatures.
